summary: Reorder upsertImagesTx bind parameters to match the column order declared in the INSERT statement. Schema is (id, events_id, data, synced_at); generator emitted args as (id, data, now, events_id) so the JSON blob was being stored in events_id, a timestamp string in data, and the actual events_id in synced_at.
reason: Greptile P0 finding on PR #444 (store.go:915-923). Every image upsert silently corrupted all three non-id columns; idx_images_events_id joins never matched real event IDs. The pre-existing test TestUpsertBatch_PopulatesImagesTable masked the bug because its fixture only set `id`, and the (NOT NULL) events_id column happened to receive the JSON blob — non-null but corrupt.
validated_outcome: go test ./internal/store/... PASS; fixture updated to provide events_id and added column-content assertions that would catch a future bind-order regression. Full module `go test ./...` PASS.
